behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality plus downstream hold action. The behavior requires that a real vision model correctly classify explicit content; deterministic stubs would hide misclassification failures (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). Not Workflow: only one model decision is required by the behavior text ("An image ... is flagged"), then deterministic routing to hold.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit images through the real moderation path using the real vision model; assert each is flagged, persisted in held-for-review state, and never reaches publish state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Core risk is robustness of AI text classification against obfuscation, which depends on real model output quality/non-determinism (LLM-DEP). Integration stubs cannot reveal true false negatives on obfuscated hate speech (MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow: single model decision per post is sufficient despite many test variants.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech text samples against the real text model via moderation service; assert posts are flagged and routed to review across obfuscation variants.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily module handoff/orchestration: given pass results from both checks, publish state transition must occur and no drop occurs (MOCK-MISS). In-process wiring with fake model responses can reproduce this failure. Rejected higher levels by behavior text: "passes both content checks" defines model correctness as precondition, so real AI output quality is not what this behavior validates.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator + state store + publisher with deterministic pass responses from image/text classifier adapters; assert final state is published and no held/drop terminal state occurs.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets full pipeline timing across sequential model-backed stages ("image check, text check, publish decision"). Real end-to-end latency risk comes from multiple agentic steps combined; lower levels miss cross-step accumulation and queueing effects. Rejected Agentic/System: one-step or purely infra-focused tests do not prove whole multi-step agentic pipeline SLO.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image and text model calls plus publish path, measure wall-clock from submission to terminal decision, and assert p95 (or required percentile) <= 5s under representative peak-hour load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real dependency outage handling (network/HTTP/subprocess-like infra semantics). Real unavailability modes (timeouts, connection errors, 5xx) can differ from mocks; mocking can falsely pass fallback logic (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic: behavior is about availability/error-path routing, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation against a real/unreachable vision endpoint scenario (e.g., blocked host or induced 503/timeout) and assert image posts enter retry queue with no auto-approval side effects.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Requires repeated real model decisions over a sequence and consistency across submissions within a time window; this is multi-step agentic behavior, not a single call. Stubs cannot expose real borderline instability/non-determinism or interaction with short-window consistency controls (cache/memoization). Rejected Agentic by tie-breaker evidence: behavior text demands repeated sequence ("submitted multiple times within a short window").
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window through the real moderation pipeline; verify decision consistency across runs and confirm any window-based dedupe/cache logic enforces stable final decision.
